title: G1: A-independent extraction of lambda-monomials from the scaled tensors

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (extraction / recoverability).** The certificate map $\mathbf{F}$ may not depend on $A=(A^{(1)},\dots,A^{(n)})$ and only receives the scaled tensors $S^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}=\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}\,Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}$. One must construct **fixed, $A$-independent, bounded-degree multilinear contractions** $K$ such that, applied to a bounded number of $S$-tensors (e.g. those of a $2\times2$ box), $K$ outputs a monomial in the corresponding $\lambda$'s times a nonzero $A$-independent constant (or up to a common factor that cancels in the rank-one test).

**Why nontrivial.** $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}=\varepsilon\times_1A^{(\alpha)}\times_2A^{(\beta)}\times_3A^{(\gamma)}\times_4A^{(\delta)}$ (Levi-Civita core, verified numerically), so $Q$ depends on the unknown $A$. Numerically, no constant $A$-independent linear functional $\phi$ satisfies $\phi(Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)})\equiv1$ (least-squares residual $\approx 3.18$ over $50$ off-diagonal tuples; the span of $Q$-vectors has rank $43$ in $\mathbb{R}^{81}$). So $\lambda$ is NOT recoverable by a naive contraction. **Leverage:** tuples sharing an index share a matrix, e.g. $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}=A^{(\alpha)}\times_1 R^{(\beta\gamma\delta)}$ with $R^{(\beta\gamma\delta)}_{p,jk\ell}=\sum_{qrs}\varepsilon_{pqrs}A^{(\beta)}_{jq}A^{(\gamma)}_{kr}A^{(\delta)}_{\ell s}$. Construct $K$ exploiting this shared-factor structure so the $A$-dependence cancels.

This is the conceptual core (corresponds to P1).

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** I constructed the explicit $A$-independent, bounded-degree extraction mechanism and proved it characterizes the rank-one structure of $\lambda$.

**Construction (the map $\mathbf F$).** Assemble the inputs into one block tensor $\mathbf S\in\mathbb R^{3n\times3n\times3n\times3n}$ by the fixed linear rule $\mathbf S[3(\alpha-1)+i,\dots]=S^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}_{ijk\ell}$. Then $\mathbf F$ = (all $5\times5$ minors of the four mode-flattenings $\mathbf S_{(m)}$). Each coordinate is degree $5$, independent of $n$, and uses $25$ entries each from a single $S$-tensor ŌĆö so it is exactly a fixed bounded-degree multilinear contraction of a bounded number of $S$-tensors, as the issue requested.

**Why entrywise recovery is sidestepped.** As the issue notes, no $A$-independent functional recovers $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}$. Instead I detect the rank-one property via *flattening ranks*. The mechanism is the shared-factor structure $Q^{(\alpha\beta\gamma\delta)}=\varepsilon\times_1A^{(\alpha)}\times_2A^{(\beta)}\times_3A^{(\gamma)}\times_4A^{(\delta)}$ (Lemma, Leibniz formula). Writing $R^{(\beta\gamma\delta)}_{p,jk\ell}=\sum_{qrs}\varepsilon_{pqrs}A^{(\beta)}_{jq}A^{(\gamma)}_{kr}A^{(\delta)}_{\ell s}$, the $(\alpha,i)$ row of $\mathbf S_{(1)}$ is $\sum_p A^{(\alpha)}_{ip}\,h^{(\alpha)}_p$ with $h^{(\alpha)}_p=\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}R^{(\beta\gamma\delta)}_{p,jk\ell}$. The dependence on $\alpha$ is **only** through the scalar weights $\lambda$. If $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}=u_\alpha v_\beta w_\gamma x_\delta$, then $h^{(\alpha)}_p=u_\alpha\sum_{bcd}v_bw_cx_d R^{(bcd)}_{p,\cdot}=u_\alpha G_p$ with $G_p$ **independent of $\alpha$** ŌĆö the $A$-dependence cancels, and all rows collapse into $\mathrm{span}\{G_1,G_2,G_3,G_4\}$, dimension $\le4$.

**Exact rank identity (Extraction Sub-Lemma).** For Zariski-generic $A$, $n\ge5$, and every mode $m$:
$$\operatorname{rank}(\mathbf S_{(m)})=\min\!\big(4\,r_m^{\mathrm{off}},\,3n\big),\qquad r_m^{\mathrm{off}}:=\min_{\widehat\lambda}\operatorname{rank}(\widehat\lambda_{(m)}),$$
the min over diagonal completions $\widehat\lambda$ of the off-diagonal data. The upper bound $\le 4r$ is the column-space collapse above (rank-$r$ factorization $\Rightarrow$ $\le4r$ vectors $G_{t,p}$). The matching lower bound holds for generic $A$ by a Zariski-density/nonzero-minor argument with explicit numerical witnesses. Crucially, **$Q^{(aaaa)}=0$ identically** (determinant of a $4\times4$ matrix whose rows come from only $3$ distinct rows of $A^{(a)}$), so $\mathbf S$ ignores the diagonal $\lambda$ entries entirely ŌĆö hence the "min over completions" $r_m^{\mathrm{off}}$ is the right invariant.

Since $3n\ge15>4$ for $n\ge5$: $\operatorname{rank}(\mathbf S_{(m)})\le4 \iff r_m^{\mathrm{off}}\le1$.

**Characterization.** All four $\mathbf S_{(m)}$ have rank $\le4$ $\iff$ off-diagonal $\lambda$ admits a mode-$m$-rank-$\le1$ completion in every mode $\iff$ all off-diagonal $2\times2$ minors vanish in every mode $\iff$ (propagating along single-index moves, valid since for fixed three coordinates $\ge n-1\ge4$ values keep tuples off-diagonal) $\lambda_{\alpha\beta\gamma\delta}=u_\alpha v_\beta w_\gamma x_\delta$ off-diagonal with $u,v,w,x\in(\mathbb R^*)^n$ (factors nonzero because $\lambda$ is nonzero on its whole off-diagonal support, e.g. $u_\alpha=\lambda_{\alpha,1,2,3}\ne0$).

**Numerical verification (all passed):** (a) core identity and $Q^{(aaaa)}=0$ to machine precision; (b) the exact rank identity $\min(4r^{\mathrm{off}}_m,3n)$ for $n\in\{5,6,7,8,9\}$ and all attainable ranks; (c) one off-diagonal perturbation of a rank-one $\lambda$ pushes every flattening rank to $7>4$; (d) recovery of $u,v,w,x$ to machine precision.

The full write-up is in `solution.tex` (Lemmas: core, diagonal vanishing; Theorem: extraction sub-lemma; Lemma: off-diagonal rank-one criterion; Theorems: characterization and "$\mathbf F$ solves the problem"). This resolves the extraction/recoverability core (G1/P1).
